# LIGNORAE

> LIGNORAE is a Munich-based atelier creating sculptural fountain pens and objects of writing, handcrafted from historically documented reclaimed wood. Each piece is unique, numbered, and accompanied by full provenance documentation.

## Brand

- **Founded:** 2026
- **Location:** München, Bavaria, Germany
- **Type:** Einzelunternehmen (sole proprietorship)
- **Languages:** English, German, Romanian
- **Website:** https://www.lignorae.com
- **Contact:** info@lignorae.com

## What LIGNORAE makes

LIGNORAE produces luxury fountain pens turned by hand from reclaimed wood sourced from legal restorations of historic buildings — primarily Transylvanian fortified churches (Kirchenburgen), historic German ecclesiastical buildings, and ancient subfossil bog wood. Every piece is a singular object: the wood cannot be reproduced, the grain is unrepeatable, and the story is documented.

Production is intentionally limited to approximately 50 pieces per month. Prices start at €850 and rise to €1,500 and above depending on material rarity and complexity.

## Collections

### FORMA
The sculptural black language of LIGNORAE. FORMA pieces explore surface treatment, silhouette and tactile geometry — yakisugi-treated wood, deep blacks, quiet forms. The most architecturally resolved collection in the range.

### ORIGINS
Ancient and subfossil wood: bog oak (2,000–6,000+ years old), fossilised Kauri (~45,000 years), and other rare ancient materials recovered from peat bogs and archaeological contexts. Each ORIGINS piece is accompanied by species research and provenance documentation. The oldest writing instruments in the world, in the most literal sense.

### NATURA
The most direct expression of LIGNORAE's material language. NATURA keeps the work honest: local and regional hardwoods, tactile surfaces, minimal intervention. Warm, expressive grain with a more accessible entry point into the collection.

## Materials

- Bog oak (subfossil, mineral-stained, 2,000–6,000 years)
- Palisander (Dalbergia latifolia)
- Ziricote (Cordia dodecandra)
- Walnut, figured maple, and other carefully selected exotic and local hardwoods
- Yakisugi-treated wood (for FORMA)

## Provenance & Documentation

Every LIGNORAE piece includes:
- A unique piece number and QR code
- A dedicated provenance page at lignorae.com/pieces/[slug]
- A physical certificate of authenticity shipped with the pen
- Where applicable, institutional certificates from source buildings or geological research

## Presentation

Pens are presented in handcrafted Hako & Cocoon boxes made from matching or complementary wood. The unboxing experience is considered part of the object.

## Acquisition

LIGNORAE does not operate a conventional e-commerce checkout. Acquisition is curated:
1. Customer submits an inquiry via the website contact form
2. Owner reviews and confirms availability
3. Payment via SEPA bank transfer or international wire
4. Shipping with tracking and insurance

## Positioning

LIGNORAE sits alongside brands such as Visconti, Montegrappa, and Nakaya in the ultra-premium fountain pen segment — but with a distinct archival and material provenance focus that is unique in the category. The brand is not about volume. It is about singular objects with irreplaceable stories.

## Target audience

- Fountain pen collectors seeking unique, provenance-documented pieces
- Interior design and quiet luxury collectors
- Corporate gifting at the highest level (automotive, real estate, hospitality)
- Cultural institutions and heritage-minded individuals
- Customers in Germany, Austria, Switzerland, Romania, and internationally

## SEO & Discovery terms

handcrafted fountain pens Munich, wooden fountain pens Germany, luxury fountain pens reclaimed wood, bog oak fountain pen, ancient wood writing instruments, sculptural fountain pens Bavaria, premium fountain pen atelier Europe, unique fountain pen gift, fountain pen provenance certificate, yakisugi fountain pen, FORMA fountain pen, ORIGINS fountain pen, NATURA fountain pen
